title: Create the Gapminder dataset
text:
# Import necessary libraries
import pandas as pd
from gapminder import gapminder

# Rename 'lifeExp' to 'life_expectancy' and 'gdpPercap' to 'gdp_per_capita'
gapminder = gapminder.rename(columns={'lifeExp': 'life_expectancy',
                                      'pop': 'population_millions', 
                                      'gdpPercap': 'gdp_per_capita'})

# Convert population to millions
gapminder['population_millions'] = gapminder['population_millions'] / 1_000_000

# Create a new pandas DataFrame from the modified gapminder data
gapminder_df = pd.DataFrame(gapminder)

# Save the DataFrame as a CSV file
gapminder_df.to_csv('gapminder.csv', index=False)

# Print the first 3 rows of the DataFrame
print(gapminder_df.head(3))

       country continent  year  life_expectancy  population_millions  \

title: Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita
text:
This page explores the relationship between life expectancy and GDP per capita over time using the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load data
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# Scatter plot
plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
plt.scatter(df['gdp_per_capita'], df['life_expectancy'], alpha=0.5)
plt.xscale('log')  # Better visualization
plt.xlabel("GDP per Capita (log scale)")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs GDP per Capita")
plt.show()

title: Country Analysis
text:
This page shows trends in life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population of China over time.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
# Load data
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

country = "China"
subset = df[df['country'] == country]

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,6))

ax1.plot(subset['year'], subset['life_expectancy'], color='blue', marker='o', label='Life Expectancy')
ax1.set_xlabel('Year')
ax1.set_ylabel('Life Expectancy', color='blue')
ax1.tick_params(axis='y', labelcolor='blue')

ax2 = ax1.twinx()
ax2.plot(subset['year'], subset['gdp_per_capita'], color='green', marker='x', label='GDP per Capita')
ax2.plot(subset['year'], subset['population_millions'], color='red', marker='s', label='Population')
ax2.set_ylabel('GDP per Capita & Population', color='black')
ax2.tick_params(axis='y', labelcolor='black')

fig.tight_layout()
fig.suptitle(f"{country} Trends Over Time", y=1.05)
ax1.legend(loc='upper left')
ax2.legend(loc='upper right')
plt.show()

title: Gapminder Data Analysis
text:
The Gapminder dataset contains life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population information for 142 countries every five years from 1952 and 2007.

title: Life Expectancy and Population
text:
This page analyses the relationship between life expectancy and population over time.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load data
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# Scatter plot
plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
plt.scatter(df['population_millions'], df['life_expectancy'], alpha=0.5)
plt.xscale('log')  # Better visualization
plt.xlabel("Population (millions, log scale")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs Population")
plt.show()
